religion_philosophy: Faiths arise from catastrophes. They promise explanations, fracture over time, and persist in corrupted forms long after their founding context is forgotten. Most people practice a blend of official faith and local tradition. Atheism is rare and carries social friction in strongly religious regions.

faith_families.origin: Agricultural. Born from the Ordinate of the Eternal Furrow era (Second Turning). Death and rebirth. The earth takes everything back.
faith_families.character: Practical, community-focused, sometimes oppressive in organized forms. Cyclical worldview.

faiths.name: The Reclaimed Path
faiths.political_alignment: Aligned with Kingdom of Durn historically
faiths.attitude_to_outsiders: Welcoming if you observe forms, suspicious if you don't
faiths.notable_figure: Brother Soren Aldrath — Prior of Durnward temple, devout and meddlesome

faiths.name: The Barefoot Compact
faiths.organization: Itinerant preachers, no permanent clergy, no temples
faiths.attitude_to_outsiders: Extremely welcoming — all are equal on the road
faiths.wealth: None institutionally — individual walkers carry nothing
faiths.notes: Irritates organized Furrow clergy considerably. Welcomed in the Free Fens more than anywhere else.

faiths.name: The Soil Below
faiths.political_alignment: None — considered marginal by mainstream Furrow
practices: Communication with the dead (ritual, not magical)
faiths.notes: Common in rural areas regardless of dominant faith — folk practice layered under official observance

faith_families.origin: Born from the Ashveil eruptions (Third Turning). The world burns, faith is survival of the ember.
faith_families.character: Austere, militaristic, often used to justify conquest. Survival as virtue.

faiths.name: The Undying Ember
faiths.political_alignment: Historically aligned with Thornsveld and northern monarchies
practices: Annual Day of Ash (memorial of the Ashveil)
faiths.attitude_to_outsiders: Wary but not hostile if you observe basic forms
faiths.notable_figure: Brother Soren Aldrath — Prior of Durnward (Thornsveld Marches)

faiths.name: The Scorched Covenant
faiths.type: Flame schismatic — believes the Fifth Turning was divine punishment for impurity
faiths.political_alignment: Used by Brekhard the Younger (The Ironmark) as legitimizing institution
faiths.attitude_to_outsiders: Suspicious of those outside the Covenant. Actively hostile to the Undying Ember hierarchy.
faiths.notes: Dominant in Ironmark territories. Used as ideological justification for expansion.

faiths.name: The Ashwalkers
faiths.organization: Loose — individual practitioners with shared beliefs, no central authority
practices: Walking through ruined places as pilgrimage
Recording the names of the dead
faiths.attitude_to_outsiders: Gentle, non-judgmental, unsettling in their acceptance of death
faiths.notes: Found near battlefields, plague sites, ruins. Tolerated by most faiths as they serve no one and ask for nothing.

faith_families.origin: Maritime origin, thalassocratic era (Third Turning). The ocean is the great equalizer, the source, the grave.
faith_families.character: Coastal communities, merchants, sailors. Relationship with death is practical and present — the sea takes people.

faiths.name: The Tidewarden Church
faiths.type: Organized Depth faith — wealthy, politically connected
faiths.organization: Cathedral-based hierarchy, banking operations, owns ~15% of Amber Coast property
faiths.political_alignment: Pragmatically neutral — outlasts rulers, invests in stability
faiths.attitude_to_outsiders: Welcoming — intolerance is bad for the transit economy
faiths.wealth: Very high — banking, property, trade fees
faiths.notes: One of three primary cathedral-complexes is in the Tidewarden Anchorage (Shattered Reach). Mediating Compact/Corsair negotiation.

faiths.name: The Drowned Compact
faiths.type: Depth faith — ancestor worship, the dead return from the sea
practices: Speaking to ancestors through tidal ritual
Specific taboos around drowning victims' treatment
faiths.attitude_to_outsiders: Community-inward — welcoming to those who respect the dead
faiths.notes: Deep roots in Shattered Reach fishing communities. Blends with Tidewarden Church practice in most coastal areas.

faiths.name: The Salt Silence
faiths.type: Depth mystics — near-silent, unsettling, extraordinary information network
faiths.political_alignment: None — but their information networks serve whoever pays
faiths.notes: Allegedly able to tell you the contents of any sealed cargo manifest in a harbor for a price. Present in every major port. Tolerated because they're useful and slightly frightening.
faiths.special: Their predictive capability is not supernatural — they maintain extraordinary information networks. They know things because they pay attention.

faith_families.origin: Based on the First Reckoning calendar and the mystery of Year 0. What destroyed Varath? Some want to prevent it. Some want to complete it.
faith_families.character: Academic to apocalyptic. Ranges from earnest scholarship to dangerous fanaticism.

faiths.name: The First Scholars
faiths.type: Academic faith — history as sacred practice
faiths.political_alignment: Politically careful — knowledge is power, they prefer to hold it neutrally
faiths.attitude_to_outsiders: Welcoming to those who value knowledge. Dismissive of the incurious.
faiths.wealth: Moderate — the Archive of Keth is three thousand years old and the most significant repository of pre-Reckoning material in the known world
faiths.notes: The Archive of Keth gives them moral authority that money can't override. Best-informed institution on the continent of Ossrath.

faiths.name: The Varathi Return
faiths.type: Dangerous cult — believe Varath should be restored
faiths.political_alignment: Considered heretical or treasonous by most powers
faiths.notes: Illegal in Keth. Present anyway, largely operating through ruin salvage networks. Extremely interested in recent Ossrath discoveries. How many actual members is unknown, which is part of what makes it frightening.

faiths.name: The Counter-Reckoners
faiths.type: Doom prophets — believe the real apocalypse is still coming
faiths.organization: Loose — individual prophets with shared framework
faiths.political_alignment: None — they consider current powers irrelevant
practices: Date-setting (the True Date of the coming collapse)
faiths.attitude_to_outsiders: Evangelical — they want you to know it's coming
faiths.notes: Perennial doom prophets. Occasionally right about regional disasters by coincidence or because they understand systemic stress. Ridiculed until something bad happens, then temporarily influential.

faith_families.character: No two regions practice it the same way. Politically marginalized, culturally persistent. Often blended with organized faiths by common people.

faiths.name: The Moor Communion
faiths.type: Organized Old Ways — the Free Fens' own faith
faiths.organization: Organized by the Drenmari See into something with more structure than raw animism but less hierarchy than Flame or Furrow
faiths.political_alignment: Aligned with Fen Lords informal governance
faiths.attitude_to_outsiders: Welcoming to those who respect the dead and the land. Patient.
faiths.notes: Moor Communion is an enormously detailed empirical system for managing life in a specific dangerous environment (the Fens), accumulated over centuries and stored in religious form. Not superstition.

faiths.name: Karn Ancestor Practice
faiths.notes: Karn clans consider their ancestral practices entirely separate from the organized faiths and are indifferent to conversion attempts.

faiths.name: The Old Ways (local variants)
faiths.type: Unorganized animism — dozens of local variants
faiths.notes: Common as a folk undercurrent everywhere. Usually practiced privately alongside official faith observance. Nobody who does it calls it 'The Old Ways' — that's a scholar's term. Locally it has no name, or has specific local names tied to specific places or ancestors.
